translation_verse: Ti Filipi yele en yeti, ‚ÄúDaana, pa'al…õ t…Ē se'em n de S…Ē la, gee bala wan tig…õ t…Ē.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
translation_verse: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, ‚ÄúMam n boi la ya saŇča kana wa woo wa Filipi, ti fum kelum ka mina mam? Se'em woo n ny…õ mam, ny…õ S…Ē la m…õ. Wani ti fum le y…õta mam yeti, ‚ÄėPa'al…õ t…Ē S…Ē la‚Äô?

english_verse: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
translation_verse: Fum ka nam ti mam boi m…õ la S…Ē la, ti S…Ē la me b…Ēna la mam? Yelehum wa ti mam t…Ēgera wa dagi mam m…õŇča yelehum, gee bu dela S…Ē se'em n boi la mam la n tuni a tuuma mam puan.

english_verse: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
translation_verse: Nam bo' mam ti mam boi m…õ la n S…Ē la, ti n S…Ē la b…Ēna la mam. Gee fum han pun ka nam, tuuma la m…õŇča zuo, fum dela fum nam bo' mam.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
translation_verse: Yelem…õŇčer…õ yelem…õŇčer…õ ti n y…õta ya wa, ti se'em woo n nam mam, me wan tuna tuuma s…õ'a ti mam tuna wa, a wan tum a pun gaŇč…õ bala, beni zuo, mam ye' la S…Ē la zi'an.

english_verse: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
translation_verse: N wan iŇč…õ s…õla woo ti ya soke b…Ēta mam yu'ur…õ puan, ti S…Ē la ny…õ p…õka dayua la puan.

english_verse: I will do whatever you ask me to do. The Holy Spirit Is Promised
translation_verse: Ya han soke mam yu'ur…õ la puan b…Ēta s…õla woo, n wan iŇč…õ d…õ. Fa'a Sia la n…Ē'pu'uleg…Ē.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúYa han n…ĒŇč…õ mam, ya wan gur…õ mam pa'aleg…Ē kankaŇči.

english_verse: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
translation_verse: Bala ti n wan soke n S…Ē la, ti a wan bo' ya Nu'pu'Ňčm…õ'…õra ayima en kpe'em paa…õ paa…õ ti a b…Ēna la ya wuuha.

english_verse: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
translation_verse: EŇča en de Fa'a Sia la n de yelem…õŇčer…õ la, se'em ti Timpaber…õ wa zuo kan to'e en, Se'ere n s…Ēi la, timpaber…õ wa zuo n…õreba ka ny…õti en, bii ba mina en. Gee yamam mi' en se'ere n s…Ēi la, a boi m…õ la ya, gee wan kelum b…Ēna la ya.

english_verse: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúN kan bah…õ ya ti ya ana wuu kiibehi la. N lebere ya zi'an me na.

english_verse: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
translation_verse: La han iŇč…õ fiiwa, timpaber…õ wa zuo n…õreba kan le ny…õ mam, gee yamam wan ny…õ mam. Mam v…Ēi la, yamam me wan v…Ēna.

english_verse: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
translation_verse: De daar…õ la, ya wan baŇč…õ ti mam boi m…õ la n S…Ē la, ti yamam b…Ēna la mam, ti mam me b…Ēna la ya.

english_verse: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
translation_verse: Se'em n mi' mam Pa'aleg…Ē duma la, gee d…Ēla a n de se'em n n…ĒŇč…õ mam. Se'em en n…ĒŇč…õ mam la, mam S…Ē la me wan n…ĒŇč…õ en, ti mam m…õŇča me n…ĒŇč…õ en, gee niira n m…õŇča pa'ala en.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, ‚ÄúLord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti Yudasi ayima, yele Yezu yeti, ‚ÄúDaana, wani ti fum wan ta nii…õ fum m…õŇča pa'ala tumam, gee dagi timpaber…õ wa?‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
translation_verse: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, ‚ÄúS…õba en n…ĒŇč…õ mam la, wan tara mam yelehum la, ti mam S…Ē wan n…ĒŇč…õ ba, ti mam la n S…Ē la wan wa'am na iŇč…õ tu b…ĒŇča zi'an ba puan.

english_verse: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
translation_verse: Se'em n ka n…ĒŇč…õ mam, ka guti mam yelehum. Yelehum wa ti ya wuna wa me dagi mam yelehum, bu dela S…Ē la n tum mam na la din…õ.

english_verse: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúN yeti ya yele ana wa woo ti n nan boi la ya wa.

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
translation_verse: Gee T…õ'…õla la kini na, Fa'a Sia la. Se'em ti S…Ē la wan tum en na mam yu'ur…õ puan la. EŇča wan pa'al…õ ya s…õla woo, gee tiiha ya s…õla woo ti mam yele ya la.

english_verse: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
translation_verse: Suma'ahum ti mam dik…õ baha ya. Mam m…õŇča suma'ahum ti mam dik…õ b…Ē'…Ēra ya. Mam ka bo'ori n suma'ahum wa wuu timpaber…õ zuo n…õreba n bo'ori ba suma'ahum se'em la. Da bah…õ ya ti ya suya daana, gee me da bah…õ ya ti dabeem tara ya.

english_verse: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
translation_verse: Ya wum mam pun yele ya yeti, ‚ÄėN yeti n kiŇč…õ m…õ, gee le lebe ya zi'an na.‚Äô Ya han n…ĒŇč…õ mam, ya wan ny…õ…õna, la mam n ye' S…Ē la zi'an la. Se'ere en s…Ēi la, S…Ē la gani mam m…õ.

english_verse: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
translation_verse: Nananawa, n pun yele ya m…õ, gee ti y…õla la baŇč…õ wa'am na, bala ti yele ana wa han ta wa'am na tum, ya wan nam mam.

english_verse: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
translation_verse: N kan le ta'am t…Ēg…õ la ya yuue, se'ere en s…Ēi la, Be'em daana se'em n p…õregeri timpaber…õ wa zuo kini me na. A me ka tari n paŇča,

english_verse: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
translation_verse: gee mam iti la s…õla ti S…Ē la bo' mam nuur…õ ti n iŇč…õ la, ti timpaber…õ n…õreba baŇč…õ ti mam n…ĒŇč…õ S…Ē la m…õ. Ihige ya ti tu kiŇč…õ.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúMam de yelem…õŇčer…õ sinsabega, ti mam S…Ē bihera sinsabega la.

english_verse: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
translation_verse: A Ňčmati yile-se'ere woo n naa…õ la mam gee ka wuna baha m…õ. Gee y…Ēreha yile-se'ere woo n naa…õ la mam gee wuna wala ti de wam zo'e zo'e.

english_verse: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
translation_verse: Yamam pun diŇč…õ ana la filam, doohe la yelehum la ti n t…Ēg…õ bo' ya la.

english_verse: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
translation_verse: Boi ya la mam, ti mam me b…Ēna la ya. Wuu yile n kan ta'am wam wala de ma'a se'em, han dagena de naa…õ m…õ la ta'aŇča la, bala m…õa ti yamam me kan ta'am wam, han dagena ya naa…õ m…õ la mam.

english_verse: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
translation_verse: Mam n de ta'aŇča la, ti yamam d…õna yila. S…õba n naa…õ la mam, ti mam me naa…õ la ba la wani z…Ē'…Ēra m…õ, se'ere zuo, la han dagena la mam puan, ya kan ta'am iŇč…õ s…õla.

english_verse: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
translation_verse: Se'em woo n ka naa…õ la mam, ba lobere en baha m…õ wuu yile ti ba Ňčmaa…õ bah…õ ti a ku'e se'em la. Yile aŇča, ba ni lageh…õ ba m…õ bah…õ bugum puan ti ba di.

english_verse: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
translation_verse: Ya han b…Ēna la mam, ti n yelehum la b…Ēna la ya, soke ya yan boti s…õla, gee la wan tum bo' ya.

english_verse: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
translation_verse: Mam S…Ē p…õka boi la, ya wam zo'e zo'e. Ňäwana ti ya wan d…õna mam zahereba.

english_verse: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
translation_verse: Wuu S…Ē la n n…ĒŇč…õ mam se'em la, bala m…õa ti mam n…ĒŇč…õ ya. Boni ya n n…ĒŇčer…õ la puan.

english_verse: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
translation_verse: Ya han gur…õ mam pa'aleg…Ē duma la kankaŇči, ya wan b…Ēna n n…ĒŇčer…õ la puan, wuu mam n gur…õ n S…Ē pa'aleg…Ē duma la se'em kankaŇči la, b…Ēna a n…ĒŇčer…õ la puan se'em la.

english_verse: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
translation_verse: N yeti ya yele ana wa woo, ti mam ny…õ…õŇč…Ē wan b…Ēna la yamam puan, ti ya ny…õ…õma pir…õ.

english_verse: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúMam Pa'aleg…Ē n Ňčwana. Ti ya n…ĒŇč…õ taaba, wuu mam n n…ĒŇč…õ ya se'em la.

english_verse: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
translation_verse: Se'em ka tari n…ĒŇčer…õ n kareg…õ gana dina wa, ti n…õra dik…õ a vom bo' a z…Ēduma.

english_verse: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
translation_verse: Yamam dela mam z…Ēduma, ya han ita mam bo' ya nuur…õ ti ya ita s…õla la.

english_verse: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
translation_verse: Mam ka yi'iri ya n yamehi y…õ'…õha, se'ere n s…Ēi la, yameŇča ka mi' ka daana n iti s…õla. Gee mam yi'iri ya la n z…Ēduma se'ere n s…Ēi la, mam bah…õ ti ya mina s…õla woo ti mam wum n S…Ē la zi'an m…õ.

english_verse: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
translation_verse: Yamam ka looe mam, gee mam n looe ya, gee tum ya ti ya kiŇč…õ ta wam wala. Ya wam wala s…õ'a n kan pu'…õ. Bala ti S…Ē la wan bo' ya s…õla woo ti ya soke en mam yu'ur…õ puan.

english_verse: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
translation_verse: N bo'ori ya pa'aleg…Ē kuna, ti ya n…ĒŇč…õ la taaba. N…õreba wan yaa…õ ya.

english_verse: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúTimpaber…õ wa zuo han yaa…õ ya, mini ya ti ka yaa…õ la mam yia, gee nyaa yaa…õ yamam.

english_verse: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
translation_verse: Ya han deni timpaber…õ wa zuo din…õ, timpaber…õ wa zuo wan n…ĒŇč…õ ya wuu a din…õ la. Gee yan dagi timpaber…õ wa zuo din…õ la, gee mam n looe ya yehe timpaber…õ wa puan la, bala zuo ti timpaber…õ wa zuo yaa…õ ya la.

english_verse: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
translation_verse: T…õri ya yelehum la ti mam t…Ēg…õ bo' ya yeti, ‚ÄėYameŇča ka gana ka daana la.‚Äô Ba han m…õleh…õ mam, ba wan m…õleh…õ yamam me. Ba han gur…õ mam yelehum la kankaŇči, ba wan gur…õ yamam yelehum la me kankaŇči.

english_verse: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Gee ba wan iŇč…õ ya yele ana woo mam yu'ur…õ wa zuo. Se'ere n s…Ēi la, ba ka mi' se'em n tum mam na la.

english_verse: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
translation_verse: Mam han ka sige ni na t…Ēg…õ ni bo' ba, ba ka tari ni tuure. Gee nananawa, ba ka tari tu ka baŇč…õ m…õ, ba be'em la puan.

english_verse: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
translation_verse: Se'em woo n yaa…õ mam, yaa…õ mam S…Ē la me m…õ.

english_verse: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
translation_verse: Mam han ka ze'ele ni ba n…õŇčan tum daalehum sebo taaba ti n…õra kan ta'am tum wa, ba ka tari ni tuure. Gee nananawa, ba pun ny…õ m…õ, gee ti ba sina gee yaa…õ mam la n S…Ē la.

english_verse: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, ‚ÄúPeople hated me for no reason.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Gee Ňčwana woo iŇč…õ ti la doohe la guleheg…Ē la n boi ba pa'aleg…Ē g…ĒŇč…Ē la puan yeti, ‚ÄėBa yaa…õ mam la yoo la.‚Äô

english_verse: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúGee T…õ'…õla la han wa'am na, se'em ti mam wan tum en ti a ze'ele S…Ē la zi'an na la, se'em n de Fa'a Sia la en de yelem…õŇčer…õ, ze'ele mam S…Ē la zi'an na la, a wan t…Ēgera la mam yele b…Ē'…Ēra ya.

english_verse: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
translation_verse: Yamam me m…õhi dela ya t…Ēgera mam yele, se'ere n s…Ēi la, ya boi la mam m…õ n pa'aleg…Ē wa pi'ileŇčo puan na.

english_verse: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúN yele ya yele ana wa, ti a gu' ya ti ya da pita luta.

english_verse: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
translation_verse: Kelehe ya, Ba wan dige ya zameheg…Ē yiya la puan bah…õ. SaŇča kini na, ti s…õba n ku ya, wan ti'ih…õ ti ban iŇč…õ bala la, ba tuni b…Ē'…Ēra la Yin…õ.

english_verse: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
translation_verse: Ba wan iŇč…õ Ňčwana doohe la ban ka mi' S…Ē la, bii mam la zuo.

english_verse: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
translation_verse: Gee mam diŇč…õ yele ya yele ana wa, ti a saŇča han ta paa…õ, ya wan t…õra ti n pun yele ya a yele. N daa ka yele ya yele ana wa pi'ileŇčo puan na la, doohe la mam nan boi la ya la zuo. Fa'a Sia la tuuma

english_verse: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
translation_verse: Gee nananawa, mam ye' l…õbera la se'em n tum mam na la zi'an, gee ya se'em ka soke mam nyaa ‚ÄėFum ye' la b…õha?‚Äô

english_verse: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
translation_verse: Gee mam n nyaa yele ya yele ana wa la, susageŇč…Ē n pir…õ ya suya.

english_verse: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
translation_verse: Gee la bala za'a, mam yeti ya la yelem…õŇčer…õ wa, ti yamam sum zuo, la ani suŇča ti mam kiŇč…õ. Se'ere n s…Ēi la, mam han ka kiŇč…õ T…õ'…õla la kan wa'an ya zi'an na. Gee mam han kiŇč…õ, n wan tum en ya zi'an na.

english_verse: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
translation_verse: A han wa'am na, a wan bah…õ ti timpaber…õ wa zuo n…õreba baŇč…õ ti ba tuue m…õ la bamam n ti'ih…õ ti be'em, tuunt…Ē…Ē vom, la gaker…õ n de s…õla la.

english_verse: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
translation_verse: Ba ka mi' be'em n de s…õla, beni zuo ba ka nam mam.

english_verse: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
translation_verse: Ba ka mi' tuunt…Ē…Ē vom n de s…õla, beni zuo, mam ye' la S…Ē la zi'an, ti ya kan le ny…õ mam.

english_verse: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
translation_verse: Ba me ka mi' gaker…õ n de s…õla, beni zuo, ba pun gak…õ be'em daana la en boi timpaber…õ wa zuo la m…õ.

english_verse: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
translation_verse: ‚ÄúY…õla zo'e zo'e boi ti n wan yele ya, gee nananawa la, la wan zo'e gaŇč…õ ya tarega.

english_verse: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
translation_verse: Gee se'em en de Sia yelem…õŇčer…õ la han ta wa'am na, a wan gu'ura ya ti ya mina yelem…õŇčer…õ la za'a. Se'ere n s…Ēi la, a ka yeti a t…Ēgera la a m…õŇča yet…Ēgum, gee a wan t…Ēg…õ la s…õla woo ti a wum Yin…õ zi'an, ti a wan muul…õ bo' ya yele s…õ'a n wan wa'am na.

english_verse: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
translation_verse: A wan bo' mam p…õka, se'ere n s…Ēi la, a wan dik…õ la s…õla n de mam din…õ, muul…õ bo' ya.

english_verse: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
translation_verse: S…õla woo ti S…Ē la tara dela mam din…õ. Bala zuo ti mam yeti, a wan dik…õ la s…õla n de mam din…õ, muul…õ bo' ya la. SusageŇč…Ē wan lebege Ny…õ…õma

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, ‚ÄúFor a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: ‚ÄúLa han iŇč…õ fiiwa, ya kan le ny…õ mam, gee la han le iŇč…õ fiiwa ya wan ny…õ mam.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: They said to each other, ‚ÄúWhat does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
translation_verse: Ti a zahereba la bas…õba y…õta taaba yeti, ‚ÄúDe vuur…õ dela deni ti a yele tu yeti, ‚ÄėLa han iŇč…õ fiiwa ya kan le ny…õ mam, gee la han iŇč…õ fiiwa ya wan le ny…õ mam.‚Äô ‚ÄėSe'ere en s…Ēi la, mam lebere la S…Ē la zi'an‚Äô?‚ÄĚ

english_verse: What is this ‚Äėlittle while‚Äô that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti ba y…õta taaba yeti, ‚ÄúYet…Ēgebuna wa vuur…õ dela din…õ ‚ÄėLa han iŇč…õ fiiwa‚Äô? Tu ka mi' en t…Ēgeri s…õla yele.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
translation_verse: Yezu baŇč…õ m…õ ti, ba boti ba soke en la se'ere, bala ti a yele ba yeti, ‚ÄúYa s…Ēheri m…õ la taaba, de vuur…õ n de se'em, la mam yeti, ‚ÄėLa han iŇč…õ fiiwa, ya kan le ny…õ mam, gee la han iŇč…õ fiiwa, ya wan le ny…õ mam la?‚Äô

english_verse: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
translation_verse: Yelem…õŇčer…õ yelem…õŇčer…õ ti n y…õta ya wa, ti ya wan kele, gee si'im, gee ti timpaber…õ wa zuo n…õreba ny…õ…õna. Ya suure wan sagum, gee ya susageŇč…Ē la wan ta lebege ny…õ…õma.

english_verse: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
translation_verse: P…Ēka han virege, la ni wuna ka m…õ, beni zuo, a d…Ēgum saŇča n paa…õ. Gee bia la han diŇč…õ lu na, a ka le t…õra too la, beni zuo, a ny…õ…õni m…õ la eŇča n tari asaala wa'an timpaber…õ wa zuo na la.

english_verse: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.

translation_verse: Bala ti P…Ē…Ēl la Banabase yee Antiyoke, la ba n…õreba bas…õba zo'e zo'e, pa'ala n…õreba la, gee muula Daana la yelehum la b…Ē'…Ēra ba. P…Ē…Ēl la Banabase welegere

translation_verse: Dabeha n iŇč…õ fii wa la, ti P…Ē…Ēl yele Banabase yeti, ‚ÄúWa'ana ti tu lebe doohe bih…õ sakereba s…õba woo ti tu doohe tihi la woo puan pa'al…õ gee muul…õ Daana la yelehum bo' ba la, la tu bih…õ ban ani se'em.‚ÄĚ

translation_verse: Ti Banabase b…Ēta ti Y…Ēn se'em yu'ur…õ deyima n yi'iri Maki la doohe ba.

translation_verse: Gee ti P…Ē…Ēl me tari Silase ti bamam me yeti ba yehe, Ti Sakereba la pu'uh…õ dik…õ en iŇč…õ Daana la zu'uheg…Ē puan.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Ti P…Ē…Ēl kiŇč…õ ta paa…õ D…õ…õbi la Lisetira tihi puan, bilam ti ba ny…õ zahera ayima yu'ur…õ en yi'iri Timoti, ti a ma d…õna Yuda buuri puan p…Ēka, d…õna sakereba la ayima, gee ti a s…Ē d…õna Giriki n…õra.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Timoti wa dela se'em ti sakereba la n boi Lisetira la Ikonium la t…Ēgera a yele suŇča.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Ňäwana ti P…Ē…Ēl b…Ēta ti Timoti wa doohe en, nyaa bah…õ ti a Ňčma, Yuuduma la n boi z…õ'se'ehi puan la zuo. Se'ere n s…Ēi la, ba daa mi' ti Timoti s…Ē dela Giriki buuri.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Ti ban dole kaara tiŇča tiŇča la, ba yeti n…õreba la ze'elego seko ti tuntuneba la kiinduma la en boi Yerusal…õm ku nuur…õ bu ba ti ba d…Ēla a suŇča la.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Ba doohe se'em kpemehe lageŇč…Ē la n…õreba ba saker…õ puan n bala, ti n…õreba me malum k…õŐÉ lageŇč…Ē la pa'ah…õ daar…õ woo. P…Ē…Ēl ny…õ la Masedoniya buraa lilegere puan

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Ti ba doohe Firigiya la Galatia s…Ē'…Ēlum la puan za'a kaa…õ, doohe la Fa'a Sia la n ka bo' ba sore ti ba muul…õ yelehum la Esiya tiŇča puan la zuo.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Ban wa'am na paa…õ Misiya tiŇča beene zuo la, ba iŇč…õ ba puti'ir…õ ti ba k…Ē'…Ēm k…õŐÉ Bitiniya tiŇča puan, gee Yezu Sia la ka bo' ba sore,

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: bala ti ba doohe Misiya kink…õleŇča sige, ta paa…õ Torowase.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, ‚ÄúCome over to Macedonia and help us!‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti yu'uŇč…Ē ti P…Ē…Ēl ta ny…õ lilegere, ti buraa n yehe Masedoniya tiŇča n bala z…õ'a la en b…õlena yeti, ‚ÄúWa'am Masedoniya na suŇč…õ t…Ē.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: EŇča n ny…õ lilegere dina wa la, ti tu ihege bilam bilam, zigera ti tu yak…õ kiŇč…õ Masedoniya, se'ere n s…Ēi la tu sak…õ ya ti Yin…õ n yi' tu ti tu kiŇč…õ ta muul…õ faar…õ la bo' ba . Lidiya teere

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Ti tu zom uureŇč…Ē, ze'ele Torowase, k…Ē'…Ēm kiŇč…õ tuunt…Ē…Ē ta paa…õ Samotirasi, yiimbeere ti tu paa…õ Niyapolise,

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: ze'ele bilam tole Filipi, kaŇča n de tink√£t…õ Masedoniya tiŇča la puan, me d…õna Aromduma s…Ē'…Ēlum. Tu yee la tinkana wa puan dabeha fii wa.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Ti vo'ohego dabeher…õ la ta paa…õ, ti tu yehe tiŇča la yiŇča, kiŇč…õ ta p…Ēra kulaa la, zi'an ti tumam ti'iha ti pu'uheg…Ē ze'a wan b…Ēna la. Tu zi'ire la bilam na la, p…Ēh…õ t…Ēgera b…Ē'…Ēra p…Ēgehi n lageh…õ na.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Ti p…Ēka b…Ēna ba puan k…õleha t…Ē, ti ka yu'ur…õ yi'ira Lidiya, d…õna p…Ēka d…õna Yin-z…Ēta, yehe Tayatira na, k…Ē…Ēha fum…Ēl…Ē. Ti Daana la yu'…õ ka suure ti ka k…õleha P…Ē…Ēl en yeti s…õla la suŇča suŇča.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, ‚ÄúIf you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.‚ÄĚ Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: KaŇča la ka yire n…õreba la za'a n ta to'e ko'om sua la, ka b…õlena tumam yeti, ‚ÄúYa han sirum ny…õ mam ti mam dela sakera Daana la puan, wa'am ya na k…õŐÉ'…õŐÉra mam yire.‚ÄĚ Ka t…Ēgera la bala tara tara ta nyaŇč…õ t…Ē. Ba pag…õ P…Ē…Ēl la Silase

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Daar…õ deyima ti tu y…õ'a pu'uheg…Ē zi'an la, tu tu'uh…õ la pugela n de yameŇča, ti kulekpa'b…õ'a d…Ēla ka, ti ka d…õna s…Ēputega, tari ligeri kina na b…Ē'…Ēra a daana la doohe la en yeti n…õreba ba y…õla la.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, ‚ÄúThese men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Pugela wa n dole P…Ē…Ēl la tumam la, ka ni ta kaah…õ m…õ bah…õ na yeti, ‚ÄúN…õrebana wa dela GaŇč…õ za'a Yin…õ la Yamehi, muula faar…õ sore la b…Ē'…Ēra ya.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, ‚ÄúIn the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!‚ÄĚ At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: A iti Ňčwana wa la dabeha zo'e zo'e. Ti a iteg…Ē la ba' nyiie P…Ē…Ēl susuya, ti a Ňčm…õreg…õ yele kulekpa'b…õ'a la yeti, ‚ÄúYezu Kiresta yu'ur…õ la puan ti n bo' fum nuur…õ y…õta fum tole yehe a puan na.‚ÄĚ Ti sankaŇča m…õa puan la, ti s…õb…õ'a yehe pugela la puan.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Gee pugela la daan duma la n ny…õ ti ban tari puti'ir…õ ti ba wan ny…õ ligeri la tole ya la, ti ba ny…Ēk…õ P…Ē…Ēl la Silase, v…õ' ba k…õŐÉ da'a puan na, tari ba wa'am kp…õ'…õnduma n…õŇčan na.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: gee wa'am na pa'ala tu n…õreba wa kiha s…õ'a n dagi ma'a bo' tumam n de Arome duma wa ti tu to'e a d…Ēla.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Ti n…õrekuuŇč…Ē la lagum pa' ba poore, zabera la P…Ē…Ēl duma, ti yelegakereba y…õ…õh…õ ba l…Ēger…Ē, gee bo' nuur…õ ti ba Ňčm…õ' ba.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Ti ban Ňčm…õ ba sagum sagum ba'ah…õ la, ba vu ba ta bah…õ la paka deo puan, gee bo' s…õba n biheri paka deo la nuur…õ ti ba gura ba suŇča.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Doohe la ban bo' en nuur…õ se'em la zuo, ti eŇča me tari ba k…õŐÉ paka deo la saraa puan ha, ta dik…õ kuto ba' ba nama tabel…õ d…Ēk√£t…õ.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: La ta paara me woo yu'uŇč…Ē sunsua b…Ēba, ti P…Ē…Ēl la Silase pu'uha gee yuuma b…Ē'…Ēra Yin…õ, ti s…õba ti ba pag…õ la ba la k…õleha.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Ba yeti ba bih…õ, ti tiŇča la tinmiiŇčo k√£t…õ n miim, ti paka deo la …õŐÉber…õ la gbiregera, ti bilam bilam ti paka kulehi la za'a yu'uh…õ, ti z…Ēt…Ē la n ba' n…õreba la za'a lorehe.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Gura la en ta ihege ny…õ ti paka deo kulehi la yu'…õ m…õ g√£'a la, ti a fooe a su'a ti a ku a m…õŇča se'ere n s…Ēi la, a ti'ih…õ ti s…õba ti ba pag…õ ba la za'a yehe z…Ē m…õ.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, ‚ÄúDon't harm yourself! No one has escaped.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Gee P…Ē…Ēl tah…õ en m…õ la k…Ēk…õ'…õŇča yeti, ‚ÄúDa p…Ēgelum fum m…õŇča gee tu woo boi m…õ.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, ‚ÄúWhat must I do to be saved?‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Gee nyaa tari ba yehe yiŇča na yele ba yeti, ‚ÄúNaa, Beni ti n wan iŇč…õ la n ny…õ faar…õ?‚ÄĚ

english_verse: They replied, ‚ÄúHave faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti ba lerege en yeti, ‚ÄúNam Daana Yezu, gee fum wan ny…õ faar…õ, fum la fum yire za'a.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Ti ba nyaa t…Ēg…õ Daana la yelehum la bo' eŇča la s…õba woo n k…õŐÉ'…õŐÉri a yire la puan la.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Ti yu'uŇč…Ē kuŇč…Ē sankaŇča m…õa puan, ti gura la tari ba kiŇč…õ ta di'ihe ba n…Ēra la, gee ti eŇča la a n…õreba za'a to'e ko'om sua kalam ka gu'um.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Beere n ta yileg…õ la, ti s…õba n gakeri y…õla la tum gureba yeti, ‚ÄúKiŇč…õ ya ta bah…õ ti buraahi la kiŇč…õ.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, ‚ÄúThe officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti gura la to'e s…Ē…ĒleŇča la ta yele P…Ē…Ēl yeti, ‚ÄúYelegakereba la tum na yeti, tu bah…õ ti ya kiŇč…õ. Bala zuo la, ya ta'am yehe nananawa la inma'ahum.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: But Paul told the police, ‚ÄúWe are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Gee P…Ē…Ēl lerege en yeti, ‚ÄúBa Ňčm…õ tumam m…õ n…õrekuuŇč…Ē n…õŇčan, gee ka ny…õ tu tuure, gee ti tu me d…õna Arome tiŇča wa n…õreba, ti ba le pag…õ tu, gee nyaa b…Ēta ti ba bah…õ t…Ē b√£√£lam? La kan iŇč…õ bala. Bah…õ ti ba m…õhi wa'am na tari tu yehe.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Ti gureba la tari yet…Ēgum la ta bo' yelegakereba la, ti dabeem ke ba, la ba wum ti ba dela Arome tiŇča duma la.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Ti ba nyaa wa'am na belum ba, gee nyaa tari ba yehe, gee nyaa yele ba ti ba bah…õ tiŇča la puan.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Ban yehe paka deo la puan na la, ba kiŇč…õ la Lidiya yire. Ban paa…õ ny…õ gee kpemehe sakereba la ba'ah…õ la, ti ba bah…õ tiŇča la puan.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: P…Ē…Ēl la Silase n wa'am na gaŇč…õ Amfipolisi la Apolonia tihi tole la, ba wa'am na paa…õ la T…õsalonika, zi'an ti Yuuduma zameheg…Ē yire b…Ēna bini.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Ti P…Ē…Ēl kiŇč…õ ta k…õŐÉ bini wuu en ni pun ita se'em la, vo'ohego dabeha at√£ ti eŇča la Yuuduma la t…Ēgera y…õla guleheg…Ē la puan,

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: ti a pa'ala ba, gee niira b…Ē'…Ēra ba ti la de la Kiresta la nameh…õ gee ihege kum puan. Gee nyaa yele ba yeti, ‚ÄúYezu kana wa, se'em ti mam muula b…Ē'…Ēra ya wa en de Kiresta la.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Ti ba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la, dik…õ ba m…õhi pa'ah…õ P…Ē…Ēl la Silase puan, ti s…õba me n de Giriki s…õba en de Yin…õ z…Ēteba la, me zo'e zo'e ta ta' la p…Ēgese'ehi n de zuo duma la me ka p…Ē'…õ.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Gee nyuure n k…õŐÉ Yuuduma la bas…õba, ti ba me ny…õ n…õreyaalehi sise'ehi n boi da'a, ti ba pa' ba poore, ti ba nyaa lageh…õ taaba bah…õ ti gbiregeri k√£t…õ sige tiŇča la puan. Ban eere P…Ē…Ēl la Silase ti ba tari ba na ze'ele n…õrekuuŇč…Ē tiŇčasuka la, ba kiŇč…õ ta zab…õ m…õ la Yason yire.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, ‚ÄúPaul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Ban ka ny…õ P…Ē…Ēl la Silase bini la, ti ba v…õ' Yason la sakereba bas…õba wa'am tiŇča la kp…õ'…õnduma n…õŇčan na, gee v…Ē…Ēla yeti, ‚ÄúN…õreba wa en dole tuleha timpaber…õ wa kpabera la, nyaa paa…õ ka'lam me na,

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: ti, Yason to'e ba ki'ih…õ a yire. Ba za'a iti la s…õla n ka dole Kaiza ze'elego la. Ba yeti, naba ayima me n boi ha ti a yu'ur…õ yi'ira Yezu.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: N…õrekuuŇč…Ē la kp…õ'…õnduma la n wum Ňčwana la, la daam ba m…õ.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Ti ban to'e bahega ligeri Yason la s…õba ti ba lagum ny…Ēg…õ ni la zi'an ba'ah…õ la, ba bah…õ ba m…õ ti ba kiŇč…õ. P…Ē…Ēl la Silase boi Beroya

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Yu'uŇč…Ē kuŇč…Ē m…õa puan ti sakereba la tari P…Ē…Ēl la Silase ta bah…õ sore ti ba kiŇč…õ Beroya tiŇča. Ban ta paa…õ bini la, ba kiŇč…õ la Yuuduma zameheg…Ē yire puan.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Ňäwana zuo la, ba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la m…õ ta ta' la Giriki p…Ēgehi la buraahi zo'e zo'e n de zuoduma.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Gee Yuuduma s…õba n boi T…õsalonika la n ta wum ti P…Ē…Ēl le muul…õ yelehum la me, ba …õ Beroya tiŇča puan m…õ, ti ba ihege wa'am bilam me na, ti ba gbirege gee tu'ule n…õrekuuŇč…Ē la.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: S…õba n tari P…Ē…Ēl kina la, tari en kiŇč…õ la zaa…õ zaar…õ ta paa…õ Atense, ti en pa'al…õ ba ba wan iŇč…õ se'em bah…õ ti Silase la Timoti wa'am na paa…õ en ka'lam ba'ah…õ la, ti ba keehe en gee lebe. P…Ē…Ēl boi Atense

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: P…Ē…Ēl n boi Atense gee gura Silase la Timoti la, a suure sagum m…õ paa…õ paa…õ, la tink√£t…õ la n pir…õ la baga la.

translation_verse: A yet…Ēgebuna ti P…Ē…Ēl me yele en yeti, ‚ÄúYin…õ wan Ňčm…õ' fum nangoonpeele wa. Fum zi' bilam ti fum gak…õ mam doohe ze'elego la, gee la bala za'a fum galum ze'elego m…õ nyaa ta bo' nuur…õ ti ba Ňčm…õ' mam?‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, ‚ÄúDon't you know you are insulting God's high priest?‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti s…õba n ze' l…õm P…Ē…Ēl la yele en yeti, ‚ÄúFum dela ani tuura Yin…õ malema kiima?‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Paul replied, ‚ÄúOh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti P…Ē…Ēl yeti, ‚ÄúMam ka baŇč…õ n suur…Ē, ti a dela malema kiima. Se'ere n s…Ēi la, guleheg…Ē la yeti, ‚ÄėDa t…Ēg…õ fum n…õreba n…õŇča daana be'em.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, ‚ÄúMy friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: P…Ē…Ēl n ta baŇč…õ ti n…õreba la bas…õba dela Sadusii duma ti bas…õba d…õna Farasiiduma la, ti a yi' kiinduma la buki yeti, ‚ÄúN suur…Ē, mam dela Farasii, mam dela Farasii dayua. Mam boi tugum wa puan wa dela, beere puti'ir…õ n ihegere la puan.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: P…Ē…Ēl n yele Ňčwana ba'ah…õ la, ti n…Ēkpe'ene lu Farasiiduma la, la Sadusiiduma la tiŇčasuka, ti lageŇč…Ē la welege buyi'.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, ‚ÄúWe don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti n…Ēkpe'en k√£t…õ ihege, ti Farasii g…Ēnmi'ineba bas…õba ihege yeti, ‚ÄúTumam ka ny…õ tuure se'ere buraa wa puan. Gee la han d…õna la sira ti Sia bii SuŇčera n sirum t…Ēg…õ bo' en?‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: N…Ēkpe'ene la n nyaa ta lebege zaber…õ la, ti dabeem k…õŐÉ p…õntiireba kp…õ'…õma doohe la ba ka gag…õ ba aahum P…Ē…Ēl z…Ēra z…Ēra la, ti a bo' p…õntiireba la nuur…õ ti ba sige ta p…õreg…õ P…Ē…Ēl wa'am p…õntiireba b…ĒŇča ze'a la puan na.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, ‚ÄúDon't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.‚ÄĚ A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Yu'ukuŇč…Ē puan la, ti Daana la wa'am na z…õ'a l…õm P…Ē…Ēl, gee yele en yeti, ‚ÄúTara suk√£ber…õ, wuu fum n muul…õ mam yele se'em Yerusal…õm puan la, bala m…õa muul…õ mam yele Arome wa puan.‚ÄĚ Ban maaheni se'em ti ba ku P…Ē…Ēl

english_verse: The next morning more than
translation_verse: Ba me gaŇč…õ n…õreba pinaahi n ku n…Ēdina wa.

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, ‚ÄúWe have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Ti ba kiŇč…õ malema kiinduma la yizuto kiinduma la zi'an ta yele ba yeti, ‚ÄúTumam k…Ē'…Ēm p…Ē la nuur…õ ti dia kan tole tumam nuuren han dagena tu ku la P…Ē…Ēl.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Bala la, nananawa, yamam la kiinduma la bah…õ ti p…õntiireba kp…õ'…õma mina Ňčwana, la a bah…õ ti ba tari P…Ē…Ēl wa'am ya zi'a na, wuu ya boti ya malum viih…õ la a yele la paa…õ paa…õ la. Gee tumam pun maahum m…õ ti tu wan ba'ah…õ en ti a pun kan paa…õ yamam n…õŇčan na.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Gee P…Ē…Ēl taya dayua wum ban yeti ba sug…õ se'em gura P…Ē…Ēl, me kiŇč…õ ta nyaŇč…õ ny…õ sore k…õŐÉ p…õntiireba b…ĒŇča zi'an la, dik…õ Ňčwana yele P…Ē…Ēl.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, ‚ÄúTake this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti P…Ē…Ēl yi' p…õntiireba n…õŇčadaana en biheri p…õntiireba k…Ēbega yele en yeti, ‚ÄúTari budibele paalega wa kiŇč…õ p…õntiira kp…õ'…õma wa zi'an gee a tari se'ere ti a ko'ohe en.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, ‚ÄúThe prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Bala ti kp…õ'…õma wa p…õntiireba n…õŇčadaana se'em en biheri p…õntiireba k…Ēbega p…õntiireba wa, to'e budibela wa, tari en kiŇč…õ p…õntiireba k…õ'…õma la ze'a na yele en yeti, ‚ÄúP…Ē…Ēl n boi paka deo puan la n yi' mam, yele mam yeti n tari budibela wa wa'am fum ze'a na, gee ka tari se'ere ti ka yele bo' fum.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, ‚ÄúWhat do you want to tell me?‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti p…õntiira kp…õ'…õma gi' budibela wa nu'o, tari ka lebe kink…õleŇča ba ma'a, ta soke en yeti, ‚ÄúBeni n de la ti fum tara ti fum yele mam?‚ÄĚ

english_verse: He answered, ‚ÄúSome men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Ti budibela la lerege yeti, ‚ÄúYuubuuri la ku la nuur…õ ti ba beere tum n…õreba fum zi'an ti fum bah…õ ti P…Ē…Ēl wa'am ba zi'an wuu ba boti ba malum viih…õ la a yele la paa…õ paa…õ la.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, ‚ÄúDon't let anyone know you told me this.‚ÄĚ Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Ňäwana ti p…õntiireba kp…õ'…õma wa bah…õ budibela wa ti ka kiŇč…õ, gee yele en yeti, ‚ÄúDa pun yele zug…Ē yiŇča me ti fum yele mam yele dina wa.‚ÄĚ Ba ta'ah…õ P…Ē…Ēl timbihega n…õŇča daana Felikise zi'an

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Maahum yire me bo' P…Ē…Ēl ti a ba', ti fum tari en la inma'ahum ta bo' timbihega n…õŇčadaana Felikise.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:

english_verse: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
translation_verse: ‚ÄúMam Kulodeyose Lisiyase n guleh…õ b…Ē'…Ēra fum zuo daana, timbihega n…õŇčadaana Felikise na. N pu'uhi fum na.

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Buraa wa ti Yuuduma la n boi ka'lam la ny…Ēk…õ ka, zigera ti ba ku ka, gee mam n baŇč…õ ti ka dela Arome tiŇčambia la, ti mam doohe la p…õntiireba ta to'e ka bah…õ.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Mam zigeri ti n baŇč…õ yelese'ere ti ba dik…õ zuuha buraa wa, mam bah…õ ti ba tari en kiŇč…õ ba kiinduma la n…õŇčan m…õ.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Mam ny…õ se'ere dela, ba zuuhe en m…õ doohe ba ze'elego y…õla puan, gee ba mam ka ny…õ s…õla ti a tuue seke ba ku' en bii ba pag…õ en.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Mam wam ti ba maaheni m…õ ti ba ku en, ti mam ta'ah…õ en fum zi'an nananawa na, gee me bo' s…õba n zuuheri en la nuur…õ yeti, ba wa'am fum ze'a na, la ba yele fum ban tari se'ere la buraa wa la.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Bala ti p…õntiireba la n to'e a nuur…õ la, tari P…Ē…Ēl bala wa'am na paa…õ Antipatirise tiŇča yu'uŇč…Ē.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Ti Yiimbeere, ti ba bah…õ ti p…õntiireba s…õba n tari yire zabera la tara P…Ē…Ēl y…õ'a gee ti bas…õba la tulege lebe p…õntiireba ba b…ĒŇča ze'a la.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Ban wa'am na paa…õ Kasariya puan la, ba dik…õ g…ĒŇč…Ē la m…õ bo' timbihega n…õŇčadaana la, gee dik…õ P…Ē…Ēl me iŇč…õ a nu'uhen.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Zuo daana wa n k√£al…õ g…ĒŇč…Ē la ba'ah…õ la, a soke P…Ē…Ēl en yehe lugese'ere na, ti en baŇč…õ ti P…Ē…Ēl yehe la Kilikiya na la,

english_verse: The governor said, ‚ÄúI will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.‚ÄĚ After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: ti a yele en yeti, ‚ÄúS…õba n zuuheri fum la han paa…õ na, n wan bah…õ ti fum t…Ēg…õ fum nuur…õ.‚ÄĚ Gee nyaa bo' nuur…õ ti ba tari en kiŇč…õ H…õr…Ēd yik√£t…õ la puan ti a ta b…Ēna bilam gee ti ba gura en.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Ňäwana wa dabeha anuu puan, ti malema n…õŇčadaana Ananiya sigena la kiinduma bas…õba, la n…õra ayima en de yet…Ēget…Ēgera ti ba yi'ira en Tetulose, ti ba dik…õ P…Ē…Ēl yele biŇče timbihega n…õŇčadaana la n…õŇčan.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Ban sam P…Ē…Ēl Ňčwana ba'ah…õ la, ti Tetulose wa p…Ēh…õ P…Ē…Ēl z…õreger…õ, y…õta yeti: Naa sukpe'ene, ‚ÄúFum zuo, suma'ahum boi tu m…õ wa'am na paa…õ zina, ti teere tee bo' tiŇča wa n…õreba wa, fum mi'ilum la zuo.

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: Tu nini ba' tig…õ la yele ana wa ti tu p…õgera a sad…Ē…Ē woo puan la zi'an woo, la pupeelum.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Gee mam ka boti n gu'um la zuo, mam beleni fum m…õ ti fum kelehe tu suhega wa gi'a gi'a la fum zu'uheg…Ē la.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: Tumam n ny…õ buraa wa, yelem…õŇčer…õ, a dela n…õrebe'o n de ny…ĒŐÉp…ĒŐÉhega bo' tiŇča wa. A dela se'em n baheri ti gbiregeri sigera Yuda buuri wa n boi timpaber…õ wa zuo zi'an woo, d…õna lageŇč…Ē seko ti ba yi'ira ba Nazarenduma la n…õŇčadaana.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Gee yamam m…õhi la han viih…õ bih…õ, ya wan wum a m…õŇča zi'an, yele s…õ'a woo ti tumam dik…õ z…õregera en wa.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Ti Yuuduma la me pa'ah…õ ba nuur…õ yeti, s…õla woo ti ba yele la n k…Ē'…Ēm bala ti ba t…Ēg…õ la. P…Ē…Ēl t…Ēg…õ gu' a m…õŇča Felikise n…õŇčan

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Timbihega n…õŇčadaana la ta yele P…Ē…Ēl ti a me t…Ēg…õ a nuur…õ la, ti P…Ē…Ēl yeti: ‚ÄúMam puur…õ pee m…õ la fum bo' mam sore ti n t…Ēg…õ n nuur…õ wa. Mam mi' ti fum bih…õ tiŇča wa la ka n…õreba dagi zina.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Gee fum m…õŇča n wum se'em la, la ka gaŇč…õ dabeha pia la ayi' n Ňčwana, ti mam kiŇč…õ zom Yerusal…õm ti n ta pu'uh…õ.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Ba ka ny…õ mam ti mam Ňčm…õ'…õri n…Ēkpe'ene la se'em pu'uheg…Ē yire la puan, bii n gbirege n…õreba zameheg…Ē yire puan bii tiŇča la puan.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Ba me ze' nananawa, ba pun kan tara se'ere ti ba pa'al…õ yeti bamam n zuuheri mam se'ere zuo wa la dela yelem…õŇčer…õ.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Gee se'ere n de yelem…õŇčer…õ ti mam wan yele fum dela, la doohe la Sore la ti bamam yeti, de dela be'em tigere, gee mam pu'uhi la tu yaabeduma Yin…õ la, gee nam s…õla woo en guleh…õ biŇče doohe ze'elego la n…Ēt…Ēgeheba guleh…õ biŇče la.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: Mam tari la beere puti'ir…õ Yin…õ zi'an. Beere puti'ir…õ se'ere ti bamam m…õhi pun sak…õ ti, tunt…Ē…Ēduma la be'em duma ihegere kum puan la wan tum.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Ňäwana zuo la, mam kpeŇčere la n m…õŇča saŇčawoo ti n tara put…õ'sun…õ bo' Yin…õ, la n…õreba za'a.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Yuuma n tole ha, ti mam yuum tari n bo'olum wa'am na ti n bo' n tiŇča n…õreba, gee me tara n kaabeg…Ē bo'olum na ti n kaab…õ.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Mam wa'am na b…Ēna malema ana puan la, ti ba ny…õ mam Pu'uheg…Ē yire la puan, ti n boi la peere malema la ba'aheg…Ē puan. N…õreba daa ka zo'e bini, bii daaŇč…Ē seko sige bini.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Gee Yuuduma bas…õba n daa ze'ele Esiya wa'am na, bamam me pun suni ti ba me boni fum n…õŇčan ka'lam, la ba yele bamam me n tari se'ere la mam.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Bii bah…õ ti n…õrebana wa yele fum n tuure n daa de se'ere ban daa tari mam kiŇč…õ kiinduma la n…õŇčan la,

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, ‚ÄúI am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Han dagena yele dina wa ma'a ti n daa z…õ'a ba n…õŇčan gee yele la. ‚ÄėDe dela n…õreba han ki ba wan le ihege la. Yele dina wa zuo ti mam z…õ'a fum n…õŇčan zina wa.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, ‚ÄúI will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Gee Felikise n pun mina Sore la yele la, malum dik…õ yet…Ēgum la ze'ele la dabeher…õ se'ere yeti, ‚ÄúLasiya n de p…õntiira kiima la han sige na, n wan bih…õ fum yele wa puan n de se'em.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Gee bo' p…õntiira n…õŇča daana se'em en biheri p…õntiireba k…Ēbega la nuur…õ yeti, guri ya en, gee bah…õ ya en ti a tara fai fii wa, da gu'uri ya a z…Ēduma ti ba da kina na bihera en gee b…Ē'…Ēra en s…õla ti a b…Ēta. P…Ē…Ēl boi p…õntiireba nu'uhen

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Ti la iŇč…õ dabeha fii wa, ti Felikise doohe la a p…Ēga Durusila n de Yuda buuri la wa'am, tum ti ba yi' P…Ē…Ēl na, ti a bah…õ en ti a t…Ēg…õ bu en ti wum a saker…õ Kiresta Yezu puan n ani se'em.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, ‚ÄúThat's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: Ti P…Ē…Ēl n ta t…Ēgera pa'ala tunt…Ē…Ēn…õ, tana fum m…õŇča tara, la gaker…õ la n kini se'em na la, ti dabeem k…õŐÉ Felikise, ti a yele P…Ē…Ēl yeti, ‚ÄúKiŇč…õ ti la iŇč…õ fii, n han le ta ny…õ fole, n wan tum ti ba yi' fum na.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Gee Ňčwana woo la, a puti'ir…õ dela P…Ē…Ēl wan bo' en ligeri. Yele dina wa me zuo ti a ni mak…õ yi' P…Ē…Ēl na zi'a la en s…Ēha.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Yuuma ayi' n ta wa'am na tole, ti P…Ēkiyose Fesetuse sooe Felikise, gee Felikise wa de gak…õ n boti a iŇč…õ ti Yuuduma la puur…õ pee la, a bah…õ ti P…Ē…Ēl kelum b…Ēna la paka deo puan.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Naba Fesetuse n wa sooe tiŇča la, dabeha at√£ n tole, ti a ze'ele Kasariya kiŇč…õ zom Yerusal…õm,

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: ta paa…õ ti malema kiima la, la Yuda kiinduma la lageh…õ yele en P…Ē…Ēl yele, ba belum en

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: gee suh…õ ti a bah…õ P…Ē…Ēl, la a bah…õ ti ba tari P…Ē…Ēl lebe Yerusal…õm na. Gee ba pun maahum m…õ ti ba wan sug…õ gura en la ba ku en sore la zuo.

english_verse: But Festus told them, ‚ÄúPaul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Ti Fesetuse lerege ba yeti, ba pag…õ P…Ē…Ēl la Kasariya tiŇča puan, la me kan yuue la eŇča m…õŇča n kiŇč…õ bini.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.‚ÄĚ
translation_verse: ‚ÄúBala zuo‚ÄĚ a yele ba yeti, ‚ÄúYa n…õheduma la sige paa…õ mam Kasariya na, ti ban tari se'ere la buraa wa la, ba wan z…õreg…õ ka.‚ÄĚ

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.

english_verse: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.

english_verse: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.

translation_verse: ti ayima me, S…õkaŇča m…õa bo' en la saker…õ, ti ayima me tibeg…Ē bo'olum yima yima,

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.

english_verse: Love is patient and kind, never jealous, boastful, proud, or

english_verse: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.

english_verse: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.

english_verse: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.

translation_verse: gee la ka boi la fii me, ti lageŇč…Ē puan, mam wan tugum t…Ēg…õ la y…õla anuu la n puti'ir…õ ti la ta'am ka'am bas…õba, nyana mam t…Ēg…õ y…õla tuha pia buuri buuri yet…Ēgum puan.

translation_verse: Bala zuo, la d…õna mam bii bamam, tumam en muul…õ se'em n bala, ti bala m…õa ti yamam me wa'am na nam. Kum Vo'ore
